design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse themed levels as Mr. Nutz, jump on/tail-strike/throw nuts at enemies, collect coins for completion bonus, reach level end to progress toward confronting Mr. Blizzard
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited lives/health per attempt) combined with exploration-based progression (hidden coins, health, and extra lives scattered throughout levels)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control an anthropomorphic red squirrel hero using acrobatic movement (running, jumping, swimming) and natural weapons (tail strikes, collected nuts) to stop a world-threatening yeti antagonist
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Genre-standard platformer with emphasis on hidden collectibles determining completion bonuses rather than speed-running; no time pressure allows methodical exploration; character-driven mascot design (squirrel chosen over turtle/parrot after designer's wife's suggestion) grounds the game in personality-first development

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with limited lives (amounts vary by difficulty setting and port); losing all health costs one life; level restart on death
difficulty.continues_system: After game over, player may continue but restarts at first stage with default lives/health and zero nuts/coins/score (penalty-based continuation)
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; death resets to beginning of current level. Password system implemented in all versions except original SNES, allowing progression resumption from stage start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty setting adjusts starting lives and health amounts; no time limits allow players to control pacing and exploration thoroughness; hidden items reward methodical play over speed

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible ammunition (nuts) gathered during play; health and extra life pickups distributed throughout levels; coins accumulate for completion bonus calculations
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six themed sequential levels unlock linearly; boss (Mr. Blizzard) encounter concludes progression; passwords enable non-linear resume points
replayability_hooks: Hidden coins scattered throughout levels create completion-percentage incentive
Bonus points awarded for quick level clears (version-dependent)
Extra lives and health hidden in levels reward exploration
Password system allows level-skip replays to optimize score

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design (six distinct environmental settings)
Character archetype conflict: anthropomorphic squirrel hero vs. yeti antagonist with magic-based world-freezing threat
Mascot characterization through sprite and animation (designed after squirrel concept approval)

boss_design.philosophy: Single climactic antagonist (Mr. Blizzard, yeti using magic to freeze world) represents narrative endpoint; boss encounter rewards player's six-level progression
notable_bosses: Mr. Blizzard (final boss, yeti antagonist attempting global ice takeover)

faction_indicators: Mr. Blizzard as singular antagonist force with world-domination agenda
Mr. Nutz as lone hero protagonist opposing magical threat
danger_signals: Environmental hazard variety across six themed levels (water sections, ice-world implications from antagonist's magic)
Boss encounter with world-altering antagonist establishes high stakes
environmental_storytelling: Six themed level environments suggest progression through varied territories toward antagonist's stronghold
Level design includes removable/bonus stages (water level cut from original, restored in GBA remake) indicating world scope beyond main path
